recap: Area of triangle = half x base x height. Circumference = 2 x pi x r. Volume of prism = cross-section area x length. Always use perpendicular height.

Q1.question: Find the area of a triangle with base 10 cm and perpendicular height 6 cm.

steps: Area = (1/2) x base x height = 0.5 x 10 x 6 = 30 cm squared.

Q2.question: Find the area of a trapezium with parallel sides 8 cm and 14 cm, and a perpendicular height of 5 cm.

Q3.question: A cylinder has radius 4 cm and height 10 cm. Find its volume and curved surface area. Give answers in terms of pi.

steps: Volume = pi x r squared x h = pi x 16 x 10 = 160 pi cm cubed.
Curved surface area = 2 x pi x r x h = 2 x pi x 4 x 10 = 80 pi cm squared.
solution.final_answer: Volume = 160 pi cm cubed, Curved surface area = 80 pi cm squared

Q4.question: A sector has radius 6 cm and angle 120 degrees. Find the arc length and area. Give answers to 2 d.p.

steps: Arc length = (120/360) x 2 x pi x 6 = (1/3) x 12pi = 4pi ≈ 12.57 cm.
Sector area = (120/360) x pi x 6 squared = (1/3) x 36pi = 12pi ≈ 37.70 cm squared.
solution.final_answer: Arc length ≈ 12.57 cm, Sector area ≈ 37.70 cm squared

Q5.question: A solid cone has radius 5 cm and perpendicular height 12 cm. Find the volume and total surface area. Use slant height = 13 cm. Give answers to 1 d.p.

steps: Volume = (1/3) x pi x r squared x h = (1/3) x pi x 25 x 12 = 100pi ≈ 314.2 cm cubed.
Curved surface area = pi x r x l = pi x 5 x 13 = 65pi cm squared.
Base area = pi x r squared = 25pi cm squared.
solution.final_answer: Volume ≈ 314.2 cm cubed, Total surface area ≈ 282.7 cm squared

summary: Area formulas: triangle = (1/2)bh; trapezium = (1/2)(a+b)h; circle = pi r squared.
Volume of a prism = area of cross-section x length; cylinder = pi r squared h; cone = (1/3) pi r squared h.
Arc length = (angle/360) x 2 pi r; sector area = (angle/360) x pi r squared.
For compound shapes, split into simpler parts and add or subtract their areas or volumes.
